hindiTranslation: हे भरतवंशियोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन  स्थावर और जंगम जितने भी प्राणी पैदा होते हैं, उनको तुम क्षेत्र और क्षेत्रज्ञके संयोगसे उत्पन्न हुए समझो ॥
englishTranslation: O best among the Bharatas, know that whatever comes into being, moving or unmoving, arises from the union of the field and the knower of the field.
essence: All forms arise from the meeting of awareness and changing nature.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: All living forms, moving or still, arise from the meeting of the field and the knower of the field.
simpleInsight: You are invited to look beneath appearances and see what truly gives rise to life.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna teaches the difference between the body-mind field and the knower within it. Here Krishna says every moving and unmoving being arises from their union, pushing Arjuna toward deeper discrimination.
modernRelevance: You watch a child sleep, a crowd rush past, a body age in the mirror. Krishna points beneath all forms and says: look for the meeting of awareness and the changing field.
translationLiteral: यावत् (whatever) / सञ्जायते (comes into being) / किञ्चित् (anything) / सत्त्वम् (being) / स्थावर-जङ्गमम् (moving and unmoving) / क्षेत्र-क्षेत्रज्ञ-संयोगात् (from the union of field and knower-of-field) / तत् (that) / विद्धि (know) / भरतर्षभ (O best among the Bharatas)
insight: It shifts attention from outer forms to the hidden principle that makes any form appear.
